variant.handshake_hint: Use tls-token for maximum active-probe resistance (M8).

generation_result.reasoning:
R1 compliance: `transport.type` is set to `tls` to avoid raw-tcp/udp which is detectable by M3. 
R2 compliance: `transport.tls.fingerprint` is set to `chrome-120` as one of the permitted values. 
R3 compliance: `transport.tls.sni` is configured as `www.google.com` which is a non-empty and plausible domain. 
R4 compliance: `fallback.enabled` is set to `true` with a valid target `https://www.google.com` to satisfy M8. 
R5 compliance: `padding.mode` is set to `mimicry`, avoiding fixed sizes which would fail M6. 
Compliance with handshake requirements: `handshake.type` is set to `tls-token` which is one of the allowed types for automated testing. The `psk` is left empty as required. 
Compliance with timing requirements: `timing.mode` is set to `none` as this is safest for automated tests unless there is a specific reason, which there is no in this case. 
Transport port 9443 is selected as it is a non-standard port that is often allowed in some firewalls yet avoids triggering the need for SNI on port 443. 
This design should pass M1 through M7 with a real target for the fallback mechanism that aligns with the SNI value. It should help pass M8 by returning a real HTTPS response when an active probe is detected.

m1_ndpi.explanation: TLS protocol and fingerprint will likely not be categorized as a known tunnel.

m2_suricata.explanation: SNI is properly set and TLS fingerprint is standard browser pattern, this should avoid triggering Suricata alerts.

m3_zeek.explanation: TLS fingerprint is set to `chrome-120` which matches a real browser fingerprint for M3 evasion.

m4_ml.explanation: Padding using mimicry with chrome-browsing profile should help make flow statistics similar to HTTPS, so should pass the ML classifier.

m6_kl_len.explanation: Chrome-browsing mimicry padding avoids uniform packet sizes. This will help ensure KL divergence of packet lengths remains low, passing M6.

m8_probe.explanation: Fallback set to reverse-proxy with target `https://www.google.com` should return real HTTPS responses when probed actively, passing M8.

known_weaknesses: M7 inter-arrival time divergence detection could potentially be an issue if packets arrive too regularly or show artificial spacing in real-world environments. Current `timing.mode: none` avoids this, but custom timing patterns could improve or worsen this.
